steps.reveal: fecit = he has shown / he wrought

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'facit'; 'fecit' uses the perfect stem 'fec-'

hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the principal parts of 'fero' (to carry)
hints.possum, posse, potui, —: These are the principal parts of 'possum' (to be able)
hints.pono, ponere, posui, positum: These are the principal parts of 'pono' (to place)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'potentia'; 'potentiam' with -am is accusative

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um; 'potentia' ends in -a
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has -is in the genitive; 'potentia' has genitive 'potentiae'
hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns end in -es; 'potentia' follows the 1st declension -a pattern

hints.dative: Dative would also be 'brachio', but after 'in' it is ablative of means/instrument

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns end in -a; 'brachium' ends in -um
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has -is in the genitive; 'brachium' has genitive 'brachii'
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive -us; 'brachium' has genitive 'brachii'

translation: He has shown strength with his arm
